sourceMovie.title: Marie Antoinette
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Marie Antoinette for the same reason it broke through in 2006: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a history rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Sofia Coppola keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The drama and history blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Les Misérables
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Marie Antoinette clicked for you, Les Misérables (2012) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and history, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Marie Antoinette?
faq.answer: Try Les Misérables, The Duchess, The Young Victoria—each shares DNA with Marie Antoinette while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Marie Antoinette?
faq.answer: Marie Antoinette (2006) is best described as Drama, History. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Marie Antoinette worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.7/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
